verses.1: Like snow in summer and like rain in harvest, So honor is not fitting for a fool.
verses.2: Like a sparrow in its flitting, like a swallow in its flying, So a curse without cause does not come to rest.
verses.3: A whip is for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, And a rod for the back of fools.
verses.4: Do not answer a fool according to his foolishness, Or you will also be like him.
verses.5: Answer a fool as his foolishness deserves, So that he will not be wise in his own eyes.
verses.6: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool Chops off his own feet and drinks violence.
verses.7: Like useless legs to one who cannot walk, So is a proverb in the mouths of fools.
verses.8: Like one who binds a stone in a sling, So is one who gives honor to a fool.
verses.9: Like a thorn that sticks in the hand of a heavy drinker, So is a proverb in the mouths of fools.
verses.10: Like an archer who wounds everyone, So is one who hires a fool or hires those who pass by.
verses.11: Like a dog that returns to its vomit, So is a fool who repeats his foolishness.
verses.12: Do you see a person wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
verses.13: A lazy one says, “There is a lion on the road! A lion is in the public square!”
verses.14: As the door turns on its hinges, So does a lazy one on his bed.
verses.15: A lazy one buries his hand in the dish; He is weary of bringing it to his mouth again.
verses.16: A lazy one is wiser in his own eyes Than seven people who can give a discreet answer.
verses.17: Like one who takes a dog by the ears, So is one who passes by and meddles with strife not belonging to him.
verses.18: Like a maniac who shoots Flaming arrows, arrows, and death,
verses.19: So is a person who deceives his neighbor, And says, “Was I not joking?”
verses.20: For lack of wood the fire goes out, And where there is no gossiper, quarreling quiets down.
verses.21: Like charcoal to hot embers and wood to fire, So is a contentious person to kindle strife.
verses.22: The words of a gossiper are like dainty morsels, And they go down into the innermost parts of the body.
verses.23: Like an earthenware vessel overlaid with silver impurities Are burning lips and a wicked heart.
verses.24: One who hates disguises it with his lips, But he harbors deceit in his heart.
verses.25: When he speaks graciously, do not believe him, Because there are seven abominations in his heart.
verses.26: Though his hatred covers itself with deception, His wickedness will be revealed in the assembly.
verses.27: One who digs a pit will fall into it, And one who rolls a stone, it will come back on him.
verses.28: A lying tongue hates those it crushes, And a flattering mouth works ruin.
